---
title: "Teaching & Open Educational Resources"
toc: true
---

EUR_DATA is built around two modules at University College Dublin that integrate
the study of EU politics with hands-on data analytics. The teaching materials
below are released as **open educational resources** under
[CC-BY-4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/): you are free to reuse
and adapt them with attribution.

## Modules

### INRL20160 — The Politics of the EU (undergraduate)

An introduction to the institutions, actors, and policy processes of the
European Union.

| Year | Syllabus |
|------|----------|
| 2025-2026 | [PDF](oer/syllabi/INRL20160_Syllabus_2526.pdf) |
| 2024-2025 | [PDF](oer/syllabi/INRL20160_Syllabus_2425.pdf) |
| 2023-2024 | [PDF](oer/syllabi/INRL20160_Syllabus_2324.pdf) |

### POL40970 — The Politics of European Governance (postgraduate)

A graduate module on European governance, combining institutional analysis with
quantitative methods and project-based work on real EU data.

| Year | Syllabus |
|------|----------|
| 2025-2026 | [PDF](oer/syllabi/POL40970_202526.pdf) |
| 2024-2025 | [PDF](oer/syllabi/POL40970_202425.pdf) |
| 2023-2024 | [PDF](oer/syllabi/POL40970_202324.pdf) |

## In-class exercises

Nine student-designed classroom exercises in the politics of European
integration, rewritten into a common instructor-ready format so they can be
rerun at any university. Each is a self-contained PDF pack: instructor guide,
timed session plan, debrief, and all role briefs and handouts as appendices.
Designed by students of POL40970 Politics of European Integration, University
College Dublin, and prepared for publication as part of the Jean Monnet
Module. Co-funded by the European Union.

### 2023/24

**The Brexit Negotiation** (negotiation, 75 minutes). A two-round simulation
of the Article 50 withdrawal talks in which students play the UK Government,
the DUP, Ireland and the European Commission over the Irish border, trade and
security. A distinctive public-opinion mechanic has each side compose a
"tweet" between rounds, drawing out how media and domestic audiences shape
international negotiation.
[Download PDF](oer/exercises/2324_g5_brexit-negotiation.pdf)

**The Ordinary Legislative Procedure: Negotiating the AI Act** (legislative
simulation, 60 minutes). Students split into the Council and the European
Parliament to legislate the AI Act's provisions on definitions, prohibited
practices and high-risk systems, moving from first positions into a trilogue
with the Commission. It makes the co-decision procedure concrete and shows how
technical drafting choices encode political interests.
[Download PDF](oer/exercises/2324_g3_ai-act-olp.pdf)

**Commission v Poland: A Rule-of-Law Moot Court** (moot court, 55 minutes). An
infringement action before the Court of Justice over a fictional
judicial-reform law tests EU rule-of-law enforcement and judicial
independence. The design pairs legal argument with a political sub-game:
intervening states are lobbied with real leverage (a looming sanctions vote,
frozen funds), exposing why enforcement so often depends on political will.
[Download PDF](oer/exercises/2324_g4_rule-of-law-moot.pdf)

### 2024/25

**Lobbying the Commission on the Glyphosate Licence** (lobbying simulation, 45
minutes). Four interest groups (an agrochemical firm, a farmers' association,
and two campaigning NGOs) lobby a mock Commissioner over the renewal of a
pesticide licence, then edit a draft policy proposal to reflect their
position. Students experience privileged access, coalition-building, and the
contested use of evidence in EU advocacy.
[Download PDF](oer/exercises/2425_g2_glyphosate-lobbying.pdf)

**Mock Trial at the Court of Justice** (moot court, 55 minutes). A
free-movement-of-goods case (a member state's ban on a product lawfully made
in another) is argued before a simulated Court, with an Advocate General,
judges, the litigating states, the Commission and a civil-society intervener.
A staged protest injected mid-hearing adds a judicial-politics dimension to
the legal architecture of the single market.
[Download PDF](oer/exercises/2425_g4_ecj-mock-trial.pdf)

**The DemocracyNowEU Forum** (debate, 50 minutes). A structured debate on the
EU's democratic deficit, legitimacy and transparency, pairing each EU
institution against a civil-society challenger across three themes before a
judging panel. Students marshal arguments for and against reform and confront
how contested the "democratic deficit" really is.
[Download PDF](oer/exercises/2425_g8_democratic-deficit-debate.pdf)

### 2025/26

**Negotiating the Treaty of Belfield** (treaty negotiation, 50 minutes). An
Intergovernmental Conference in which eight member-state delegations negotiate
a fictional amendment to the Common Security and Defence Policy, funded by
reallocating sustainability money to defence. The unanimity rule means any
single veto blocks adoption, so students learn coalition-building and the
power of the status quo first hand.
[Download PDF](oer/exercises/2526_g1_treaty-of-belfield.pdf)

**The Annual EU Budgetary Procedure** (budget simulation, 45-50 minutes). The
Council and the European Parliament negotiate the annual budget across four
headings, each group working from a position brief with defined red lines.
Different voting rules, the shadow of each institution's veto, and the
fallback to last year's budget reveal the institutional forces that shape real
budget outcomes.
[Download PDF](oer/exercises/2526_g7_eu-budget-procedure.pdf)

**Judicial Politics: A Mock Appeal at the Court of Justice** (moot court, 50
minutes). Students stage an appeal before the Court of Justice arising from an
enforcement dispute between the European Commission and a large technology
company under the Digital Services Act and Digital Markets Act. Alongside the
litigating parties and an intervening member state, two Advocate General teams
hold an information session and cross-examine before delivering an opinion,
and the Court issues a reasoned ruling. The exercise exposes the interplay of
law, expertise and politics in EU tech regulation and the distinctive role of
the Advocate General.
[Download PDF](oer/exercises/2526_g4_cjeu-meta-moot.pdf)

## Working with the data in teaching

The [Commission Formation dataset](data.qmd) is designed for classroom use. The
worked example notebook in the repository shows how to load and explore the
tables:
[exploration.ipynb](https://github.com/Connected-Politics-Lab/EUR_DATA/blob/main/datasets/commission_formation/notebooks/exploration.ipynb).
